name: MITRE.LIN.T1546.004.RULE Event Triggered Execution: Unix Shell Configuration Modification
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-w /etc/profile.d/ -p w -k siem-bashrc
-w /etc/profile -p w -k siem-bashrc
-w /etc/shells -p w -k siem-bashrc
-w /etc/bashrc -p w -k siem-bashrc
-w /etc/csh.cshrc -p w -k siem-bashrc
-w /etc/csh.login -p w -k siem-bashrc
-w /root/.bashrc -p w -k siem-bashrc
-w /root/.bash_profile -p w -k siem-bashrc
-w /home/<user>/.bashrc -p w -k siem-bashrc
-w /home/<user>/.bash_profile -p w -k siem-bashrc

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-bashrc
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1546.004.RULE Event Triggered Execution: Unix Shell Configuration Modification on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-bashrc
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1546.004.RULE Event Triggered Execution: Unix Shell Configuration Modification

Event description: Adversaries may abuse these shell scripts by inserting arbitrary shell commands that may be used to execute other binaries to gain persistence.
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1546/004
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
